description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
Add SpecLedEx.Realization.EffectiveBinding.expand_implications/1 — pure idempotent function on a binding map (string-keyed tier => list of MFA-or-bare-module strings). For every entry under 'implementation', ensure the same entry appears in 'api_boundary' (deduplicated, sorted lexicographically). Other tiers untouched. Implementation is one-way; api_boundary-only entries do NOT seed implementation. Idempotent.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: effective_binding_test.exs covers idempotency, one-way semantics, dedup, sort, empty-input branches, both-tiers populated case, other-tiers-untouched.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: spec requirements specled.realized_by.implication_one_way and specled.realized_by.implication_invoked_per_layer (the 'invoked per-layer' part is verified at S5).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex (S5)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex (S3)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S4)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (S4/S5)
- .spec/**
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding_test.exs

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT modify for_requirement/2's signature or behavior.
- The orchestrator wiring of expand_implications is S5's job, not this stage's.
- No detector wiring; no orchestrator wiring.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Tests added (in SpecLedEx.Realization.EffectiveBindingTest, "expand_implications/1" describe block):
- "every implementation entry also appears under api_boundary"
- "api_boundary-only entries do NOT seed implementation (one-way)"
- "is idempotent: calling twice produces the same result"
- "deduplicates api_boundary when subject already lists implementation entry there"
- "api_boundary list is sorted lexicographically"
- "empty input returns an empty map"
- "no implementation tier leaves api_boundary untouched (no sort, no dedup forced)"
- "no api_boundary tier yet but implementation present seeds api_boundary"
- "tiers other than api_boundary and implementation are untouched"
- "both tiers populated: api_boundary becomes the union, sorted and deduped"
- "atom tier keys are normalized to strings"
- "bare-module entries flow through the implication identically to MFA strings"

Verification stubs flipped: none.
The verification block in .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md grouping `implication_one_way` + `implication_invoked_per_layer` also covers `implication_drift_both_tiers`, `implication_dangling_once`, and `implication_amplification_dedup` — those belong to S4/S5. Flipping the stub now would falsely promise tests for those ids. The ticket also lists `.spec/**` under Out of Scope (files), so the stub flip is deferred to whichever stage finishes coverage of that block (S5 per the plan).

Summary: Added pure idempotent `EffectiveBinding.expand_implications/1` that takes a string-keyed binding map and ensures every entry under `"implementation"` also appears under `"api_boundary"`, deduplicated and sorted lexicographically. Implementation is one-way: api_boundary-only entries are not seeded into implementation. Other tiers (`expanded_behavior`, `use`, `typespecs`) pass through untouched. `for_requirement/2`'s signature and behavior are unchanged. No detector or orchestrator wiring (S5's job).

Local checks:
- `mix compile`: clean
- `mix test test/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding_test.exs`: 19 tests, 0 failures (7 pre-existing + 12 new)
- `mix test` (full suite): 2 properties, 670 tests, 0 failures
- `mix spec.validate`: 35 findings (down from 39 on epic base; remaining belong to other stages)
- Diff scope check: only `lib/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding.ex` and `test/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding_test.exs` touched (both in Allowed touches; nothing under .spec/**, orchestrator, canonical, api_boundary, or implementation tiers).

VERIFY: PASS

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 (the verification block grouping `implication_one_way` + `implication_invoked_per_layer` also covers `implication_drift_both_tiers`, `implication_dangling_once`, `implication_amplification_dedup` (S4/S5). `.spec/**` is also Out of Scope (files), so the stub flip is correctly deferred to S5. No S2-only stub exists to run.)
- Requirements with passing tests: 2/2 (implication_one_way, implication_invoked_per_layer; the orchestrator-applied "per-layer" portion of implication_invoked_per_layer is explicitly deferred to S5 per the ticket's Verification language)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none (for_requirement/2 untouched; no orchestrator wiring; no detector wiring)
- Project verification: mix test passes (2 properties, 670 tests, 0 failures); mix compile clean; mix spec.check fails with 17 errors, but all 17 belong to other-stage requirements (bare_module_*, redundant_dup_warning, dedup_check_shared_seam, silent_seed*, implication_drift_both_tiers, implication_dangling_once, implication_amplification_dedup, binding_ref_inferred_no_leak, dedup_realized_by_*) — none are S2's two requirements, which now have @tag spec coverage and no longer surface as findings.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- `expand_implications/1` is pure, accepts atom-keyed and string-keyed maps (normalizes to strings), wraps non-list values via `List.wrap/1`, returns input untouched when implementation tier is empty/missing, and uniqs+sorts api_boundary on the merge path. The non-map fallthrough clause `def expand_implications(other), do: other` is benign — `for_requirement/2` always returns a map so the public surface only sees maps.
- 12 new ExUnit tests under `describe "expand_implications/1"` cover: implication seeding, one-way semantics, idempotency, dedup of pre-existing api_boundary entries, lex sort, empty input, no-implementation-tier passthrough, no-api_boundary-tier seeding, other-tiers untouched (expanded_behavior/use/typespecs), both-tiers union, atom-key normalization, and bare-module entries flowing identically to MFA strings. Each requirement has multiple tests that would fail under the obvious mutations (drop the impl→api copy; mutate the implementation list; skip dedup; skip sort).
- Diff scope: only lib/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding.ex and test/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding_test.exs touched. No `.spec/**`, no orchestrator/canonical/api_boundary/implementation modules, no detector code. Confirmed by `git diff abeee1ea..HEAD --name-only`.
- Source-claim trace: spec surface block lists `SpecLedEx.Realization.EffectiveBinding.expand_implications/1` under both api_boundary and implementation tiers. The function exists at lib/specled_ex/realization/effective_binding.ex:55 with the documented signature and contract. No call sites in lib/ yet — that's S5's job per the spec text "the orchestrator shall apply ..." and per the ticket's UNBLOCKS pointing at specled_-701.6 (S5 orchestrator wiring).
